body:
In Alameda County, the cost to replace garage door springs typically ranges between **$300 and $700** total for parts and professional labor.

Because the Bay Area has a higher cost of living and higher labor rates, prices tilt toward the higher end of national averages.

Here is a breakdown of what you can expect to pay:

## Cost Breakdown by Spring Type

The final bill depends mostly on the mechanism your garage door uses:

  * **Torsion Springs (Most Common):** These sit on a metal bar directly above your closed garage door. They are under immense tension and last longer. Replacing a pair of torsion springs usually costs between **$450 and $700**.

  * **Extension Springs:** These run along the upper tracks on the sides of the door. They are cheaper and easier to replace, usually costing between **$300 and $450** for a pair.

## Local Pricing & Factors to Watch Out For

  * **Always Replace Both:** Even if only one spring is broken, companies will (
